danger_signals.visual_form: Armed terrorists and hostile combatants in stage environments
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Non-hostile characters present in stages (mall, city, office)
danger_signals.meaning: Collateral damage risk; three civilian kills force stage restart

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon ammo counters deplete; pistol requires reload every 15 shots
Player health bar visible during gameplay
danger_signals.meaning: Accumulating damage from enemy fire indicates growing danger

safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration and temporary mitigation of danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Three additional firearms available per stage (Uzi, shotgun, flamethrower)
safety_signals.meaning: Escalating player capabilities reduce threat perception

safety_signals.visual_form: Architectural elements in stages (chateau, mall, dock, refinery)
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical positioning opportunities implied by stage design

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Overhead-view movement through civilian-occupied and industrial spaces

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed combatants deployed across nine distinct geographic locations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in chateau, mall, park, subway, dock, Chinese city, oil refinery, highway, office building
Mall, park, subway, Chinese city, office building settings indicate civilian concentration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Combat-heavy environments with armed enemy deployment in civilian areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Harry must neutralize threats while protecting non-combatants; three-kill civilian penalty enforces non-lethal restraint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression from urban spaces (chateau, mall, park) to strategic/industrial targets (dock, refinery, highway)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Weapon upgrade availability and ammo scarcity increases tactical complexity; player must manage resource constraints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final office building stage representing infiltration of terrorist command center
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Culminating confrontation with Salim Abu Aziz plot foiling

environmental_storytelling.example: Nine distinct geographic stages (chateau → mall → park → subway → dock → Chinese city → oil refinery → highway → office) create spatial narrative arc from initial infiltration to final confrontation
Movie stills mark beginning of each level and game conclusion, grounding gameplay in film narrative context
Stages vary in non-combatant presence (high in mall/park/subway; low in industrial/remote locations), indicating threat escalation and mission scope changes
Chateau (infiltration), mall (urban assassination), dock (logistics hub), refinery (facility destruction), office (command center) suggest distinct operational objectives

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can maintain aim direction while shooting and executing diving shoulder roll to evade enemy fire
Pistol reload requirement (every 15 shots) and limited ammo for other weapons create vulnerability moments
Deploy across stage environments with projectile-based attacks; can be eliminated via various weapons
Stationary or mobile obstacles; three kills trigger forced stage restart, signaling player accountability mechanic

notes: True Lies SNES uses top-down run-and-gun mechanics with strong civilian protection narrative imperative. Visual communication of world-state relies heavily on environmental variety (urban to industrial), NPC presence density, and weapon availability progression. The game enforces moral constraint (civilian casualty limit) as core mechanical feedback, translating film's espionage theme into gameplay tension. Danger signals are primarily enemy presence and resource scarcity; safety signals are weapon/health pickups. Faction distinction is minimal visually (limited color/sigil documentation), relying instead on spatial positioning and NPC type variation.
